method: LLM visual digitization vs FDA-reported PK parameters

validations.notes: Same units, clean line chart, easy to read data points. Tmax difference may reflect figure showing later-peaking FDC-XR formulation vs median in table.

Unit conversion required (nmol/L to ng/mL, MW=450.9). Tmax discrepancy: figure shows arithmetic mean peak at 3h, table shows median Tmax 1.5h. Geometric vs arithmetic mean and individual vs population Tmax contribute to differences.

validations.reported_source: Table 2, Visit 7 Day 105, Arithmetic Mean, All Doses Combined
validations.notes: High variability drug (CV 75.5%). Three overlapping visit curves make precise reading difficult. Reported values are arithmetic mean with SD 584, so large uncertainty. Table values are for V7 (Day 105) across ALL dose levels; figure also shows V4b and V2. BID dosing creates double-peak profile.

key_findings: Clean line charts with clear axes yield <5% Cmax error
Unit conversion (nmol/L to ng/mL) introduces additional uncertainty
Highly variable drugs with overlapping curves yield ~14% error
Tmax estimation is less reliable than Cmax (median in tables vs mean in figures)
Geometric LS mean (tables) vs arithmetic mean (figures) contributes to systematic differences
